# Trinity Strategy Implementation Runbook — Cash Flow Tracker
**Generated by:** CFO Agent
**Date:** 2026-03-29
**Purpose:** Operational cash flow tracking, weekly reviews, budget management
**Audience:** CFO, COO (daily operations)

---

## Executive Checklist: Pre-Launch (By March 30)

### Week 1 Startup (March 29–30, 36-hour sprint)

**Digital Products Setup (CEO/COO ownership, CTMO support)**
- [ ] Gumroad account created + verified
- [ ] Canva Pro subscription activated ($13/month)
- [ ] First digital product (template/guide) uploaded + pricing set
  - Moderate scenario: 2 products ready, $20 avg price
  - Conservative scenario: 1 product, $15 price
  - Aggressive scenario: 3 products, $25 avg price
- [ ] Payment method verified (Stripe/PayPal)
- [ ] First 3 customers (friends/beta testers) to validate flow

**Freelance Writing Setup**
- [ ] Writing platform chosen (Substack, Medium, own blog, Upwork profile)
- [ ] Portfolio/samples organized
- [ ] First 2–4 articles outlined
- [ ] Client outreach list created (targets: SaaS blogs, tech publications, AI niche)
- [ ] Pricing confirmed ($200–300/article for Moderate scenario)

**Affiliate Marketing Setup**
- [ ] WordPress site live (hosting + domain)
- [ ] 3–5 pillar articles drafted (AI tools, productivity, SaaS)
- [ ] Affiliate accounts created (ConvertKit, Zapier, GetResponse, etc.)
- [ ] Affiliate links in posts tested
- [ ] SEO basics configured (titles, meta descriptions, internal links)

**Automation & Tracking (CTMO ownership)**
- [ ] PostgreSQL database schema ready (revenue_transactions table)
- [ ] n8n workflows Phase 1 configured:
  - Gumroad → revenue table (daily)
  - Freelance income → revenue table (weekly)
  - Affiliate clicks/sales → revenue table (daily)
- [ ] Revenue dashboard accessible (CFO reads daily)
- [ ] Email alerts configured for milestones ($100, $500, $1K, $5K)

**Budget Allocation ($1,000 seed)**
- [ ] $350 → Digital products (Canva, Gumroad, design assets)
- [ ] $200 → Freelance writing (research, templates, tools)
- [ ] $200 → Affiliate/WordPress (6-month hosting, SEO tools trial)
- [ ] $150 → Automation (PostgreSQL, n8n, integrations)
- [ ] $100 → Contingency reserve
- [ ] **Total allocated:** $1,000 ✅

---

## Decision Gate 1: April 30 Evaluation

### Decision Framework

**CONTINUE with current scenario IF:**

**Conservative Path:**
- [ ] April net profit ≥ $200 AND
- [ ] Digital products ≥ 2 sales AND
- [ ] Freelance writing ≥ 1 article published

**Moderate Path (Recommended):**
- [ ] April net profit ≥ $600 AND
- [ ] Digital products ≥ 8 sales AND
- [ ] Freelance writing ≥ 2 articles published AND
- [ ] Affiliate traffic ≥ 200 clicks

**Aggressive Path:**
- [ ] April net profit ≥ $1,200 AND
- [ ] Digital products ≥ 15 sales AND
- [ ] Freelance writing ≥ 4 articles published AND
- [ ] Affiliate conversions ≥ 5

---

### Escalation / De-escalation Rules

**ESCALATE TO NEXT SCENARIO IF:**
- [ ] Current scenario net profit >150% of target for 2 consecutive weeks
- [ ] Both non-writing streams growing >20% week-over-week
- [ ] Capacity available for additional work (CEO/COO assessment)

**Example:** Running Moderate scenario; April profit = $1,400 (>150% of $800 target)
→ Escalate to Aggressive for May; hire contractor support; add 3rd product

---

**DE-ESCALATE TO PREVIOUS SCENARIO IF:**
- [ ] Current scenario net profit <50% of target
- [ ] 2+ streams show declining week-over-week
- [ ] Execution quality suffering (missed deadlines, poor content)

**Example:** Running Aggressive scenario; April profit = $800 (<50% of $1,689 target)
→ De-escalate to Moderate; focus on 2 products + 7 articles/month; pause Aggressive scaling

---

## May Execution Plan (Contingent on April Results)

### If Moderate Path Achieved (April ≥ $600 net)

**Primary Objectives:**
- [ ] Scale digital products from 8→50 units/month
- [ ] Increase freelance writing from 2→7 articles/month
- [ ] Build affiliate audience (target 5K+ monthly views by May 31)

**Resource Allocation:**
- Digital products: 10 hrs/week (design, pricing tests, marketing)
- Freelance writing: 15 hrs/week (research, write, publish)
- Affiliate marketing: 8 hrs/week (SEO optimization, promotion)

**Hiring Decision:**
- [ ] If freelance demand >8 articles/mo, hire contractor ($300–400/mo for 4 articles)
- [ ] If digital product design bottleneck, use Canva templates ($13/mo) + freelancer ($200 for 5 designs)

**May Revenue Target: $2,500+ net**

---

### If Conservative Path Achieved (April $200–$600)

**Primary Objectives:**
- [ ] Validate digital product offering (analyze sales data)
- [ ] Publish 4 articles (increase from 2 to 3–4/week cadence)
- [ ] Drive organic affiliate traffic (SEO-focused content)

**Resource Allocation:**
- Digital products: 7 hrs/week (focus on 1 product; iterate pricing/messaging)
- Freelance writing: 12 hrs/week (build content library)
- Affiliate marketing: 8 hrs/week (organic growth, no paid ads)

**Hiring Decision:**
- [ ] Hold on hiring; bootstrap with existing capacity
- [ ] Use free/low-cost tools (Ahrefs free, ChatGPT for research)

**May Revenue Target: $1,200+ net**

---

### If Aggressive Path Achieved (April ≥ $1,200)

**Primary Objectives:**
- [ ] Scale digital products to 120+ units/month (production + marketing)
- [ ] Maximize freelance writing output (10–12 articles/month) — HIRE SUPPORT
- [ ] Accelerate affiliate marketing (organic + early paid ads)

**Resource Allocation:**
- Digital products: 15 hrs/week (production, marketing, A/B testing)
- Freelance writing: 20+ hrs/week (manage team + write premium articles)
- Affiliate marketing: 10 hrs/week (promotion, analytics, optimization)

**Hiring Decision (CRITICAL):**
- [ ] Hire 1 freelance writer ($300–400/mo for 4 articles) ASAP
- [ ] Consider design contractor ($200–300/mo for 10+ product designs)
- [ ] Explore paid affiliate promotion ($200–500/mo budget)

**May Revenue Target: $5,000+ net**

## 6-Month Cash Flow Projection (Rolling Forecast)

### Update Monthly (To Replace Initial Forecast with Actual Results)

**Format: Each month, replace forecast with actual; project remaining 3 months forward**

```
=== APRIL 30 UPDATE ===

CUMULATIVE RESULTS (Apr actual)
├─ April actual: $___
├─ April forecast remainder: [remaining days × daily avg]
└─ April final: $___

ROLLING 6-MONTH FORECAST (May–Sep)
├─ May forecast: $__ (based on Apr velocity + planned scaling)
├─ June forecast: $__ (with assumptions)
├─ July forecast: $__ (target: $10K cumulative by July 10)
├─ August forecast: $__
├─ September forecast: $__
└─ Total 6-month: $__

SCENARIO EVALUATION
├─ Conservative still viable? [YES / NO / REVERT IF]
├─ Moderate on track? [YES / NO / ESCALATE IF]
├─ Aggressive possible? [YES / NO / WHEN]
└─ Recommendation: [Stay / Escalate / Revert]

NEW DECISIONS REQUIRED?
├─ Hiring? [YES / NO / ROLE]
├─ Budget reallocation? [YES / NO / WHERE]
├─ Product pivot? [YES / NO / WHICH]
└─ Timeline adjustment? [YES / NO / REASON]
```

## Revenue Recognition Policy

### When to Record Revenue (Accounting Standards)

**Digital Products:**
- Record: When payment received (Gumroad instant; 2–3 day settlement)
- Deduct: 10% Gumroad commission
- Recognize: Date of payment in database

**Freelance Writing:**
- Record: When article accepted/published (not upon invoice)
- Deduct: 2% payment processor fees
- Recognize: Date of publication (or payment if earlier)

**Affiliate:**
- Record: When commission paid by affiliate platform
- Deduct: 0% (already net commission from platform)
- Recognize: Date of payout (usually 30 days after conversion)

**Exception:** For cash flow forecasting, record affiliate as "pending" on sale date, "confirmed" on payout date.

---

## End-of-Month Close Checklist

**Perform by 5 PM on last day of each month:**

- [ ] Export all transactions from Gumroad, Stripe, PayPal
- [ ] Verify affiliate payouts received (or pending date recorded)
- [ ] Tally freelance article completions + payments
- [ ] Calculate total commissions/fees deducted
- [ ] Update PostgreSQL revenue table with all transactions
- [ ] Reconcile n8n automated entries vs. manual verification
- [ ] Calculate net profit for the month
- [ ] Compare actual vs. forecast by stream
- [ ] Document variances (>10% needs explanation)
- [ ] Write monthly close summary (revenue, decisions, outlook)
- [ ] Present to board at standup
- [ ] Archive all receipts/documentation
